zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Adams於1603年7月出生在英國英格蘭倫敦布倫登時，她的父親Jacob Adams27歲，母親Mary Adams7歲。她於1624年11月16日與Richard Burt在英國米德萊可堂爾 哈姆勒次結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1637年1月在英國英格蘭素可享年33歲，埋葬在英國素可庫費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Adams于1603年7月出生在英国英格兰倫敦布倫登时，她的父亲Jacob Adams27岁，母亲Mary Adams7岁。她于1624年11月16日与Richard Burt在英国米德萊可堂爾 哈姆勒次结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1637年1月在英国英格兰素可享年33岁，埋葬在英国素可庫費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Adams
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Santo, Giovanna, Nicola, Aldo, Baldassare, Carmelo, Carmino.
Italian: from the personal name Adamo, the Italian form of Adam .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Adams was born in July 1603, in Blendon, London, England, United Kingdom, her father, Jacob Adams, was 27 and her mother, Mary Adams, was 7. She married Richard Burt on 16 November 1624, in Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in January 1637, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 33, and was buried in Cuckfield, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Adams est née en juillet 1603, à Blendon, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Jacob Adams, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Mary Adams, avait 7 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Burt le 16 novembre 1624, à Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en janvier 1637, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 33 ans, et a été enterrée à Cuckfield, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Adams im Juli 1603 in Blendon, London, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jacob Adams 27 und ihre Mutter Mary Adams 7. Sie hat am 16. November 1624 in Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, England Richard Burt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im Januar 1637 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cuckfield, Sussex, England begraben.

Lei sposato Richard Burt il 16 novembre 1624, nel Tower Hamlets, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel gennaio 1637, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 33 anni, e fu sepolta a Cuckfield, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Adamsが1603年7月にイギリスイギリスロンドンブレンドンで生まれた時，父Jacob Adamsは27歳，母Mary Adamsは7歳であった。1624年11月16日にイギリスミッドレセックストウエル ハムレツでRichard Burtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1637年1月にイギリスイギリススセックスで33歳で死去し，イギリススセックスクウクフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Adams(이)가 1603년 7월 영국 잉글랜드 런던 블렌던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jacob Adams(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Adams(은)는 7세였다. 그녀는 1624년 11월 16일 영국 미들레세스 타워 함레츠에서 Richard Burt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1637년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 33세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 cck필드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Adams родилась июль 1603 г. в местности блендон, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Jacob Adams было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Adams, 7. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Burt 16 ноября 1624 г. в местности тоуир хамлец, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла январь 1637 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 33 лет и была похоронена в местности куккфиелд, суссекс, Англия.
